elements.title: Victoria Hall
elements.contents: ... that Victoria Hall (pictured), a National Historic Site of Canada, was gutted by fire weeks after its centennial?

elements.title: Mary Bach
elements.contents: ... that consumer rights activist Mary Bach successfully sued Walmart in 2011 over a matter of two cents?

elements.title: works of Edith Maryon
elements.contents: ... that the works of Edith Maryon include sculptures of a witch on a broomstick, a dance from Peer Gynt, and a child sucking its thumb?

elements.title: a Nintendo 3DS game
elements.contents: ... that a Nintendo 3DS game was originally planned to be available for six months, but was still available almost seven years later until the 3DS eShop closed in March 2023?

elements.title: Maggi Rubenstein
elements.contents: ... that Maggi Rubenstein has been called the "godmother of sex education"?

elements.title: the first season of Malcolm in the Middle
elements.contents: ... that Bryan Cranston wore a suit of 10,000 bees for a scene in the first season of Malcolm in the Middle, but was stung only once?

elements.title: Sabra Field
elements.contents: ... that a 1991 US postage stamp based on a print by Sabra Field sold more than 60 million copies?

elements.title: a monument to something that never happened
elements.contents: ... that Poland has a monument to something that never happened?
